rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in implementation scope only when binding implementation-level features or outputs.
Preserve AADL component type and implementation structure when inserting annexes.
generic_rules: Use component type scope for visible input-output contracts; use implementation scope for bindings tied to subcomponents.
Ensure all AGREE variable references correspond to visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE scalar types are int, real, bool — never use integer, boolean, or other variants.
Guarantees in implementation scope must reference only existing outputs or auxiliary variables, not subcomponent ports directly.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope unless bound to a declared output or auxiliary variable.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes without proper feature mapping.
